summary: Iter102 — Phase B (AI Pricing Grid via Claude Sonnet 4.5) + Phase C (Admin Transport Overview Dashboard) backend tested exhaustively. 29/29 new tests pass (one 502 cold-start flake on first ingress hit recovered immediately on retry). Regression: full iter99 + iter100 + iter101 suite (60 tests) still GREEN. Phase B endpoints behave per spec including auto-archive of the previous active row when a new one is validated (partial UNIQUE index ux_pricing_active_per_country_vt enforces it). /api/transport/estimate now correctly returns currency + pricing_source ('grid' vs 'default_xaf'). The real Claude Sonnet 4.5 call via emergentintegrations succeeded on every run (rationale populated, base_fare/per_km numeric).

minor.issue: First request after a long ingress idle returns 502 once before recovering (Kubernetes preview cold-start, not application code). Retest passed in 3.7s. Not iter102-specific.

critical_code_review_comments: transport.py /admin/overview reports `revenue.currency: 'XAF'` hard-coded even when fares were collected from grids in EUR/NGN/etc — there's already a TODO comment ('mixed; UI may add a note. Phase B per-country WIP'). For mixed-country deployments this aggregate is misleading. Suggest grouping by currency (revenue: { XAF: {gross,commission}, EUR: {...} }) or at minimum returning revenue per pricing_grid.currency seen in the window.

Phase C overview revenue uses fare_final * 0.15 to compute commission. This is hard-coded and duplicates the COMMISSION constant from the top of transport.py. If commission rate ever changes per country (Phase 5+ surge / per-country plans), this query would silently lie. Better: store commission_pct on the pricing_grid row and SUM(fare_final * commission_pct).

/admin/pricing/{id}/validate uses `FOR UPDATE` on the target row but the previous active row is updated with a plain UPDATE — there's a tiny race (admin A validates pid_X for CM/standard; admin B validates pid_Y for CM/standard at the exact same time → both archive the previous active and both try to flip to active, second one hits the partial unique index and surfaces a 500 (raw asyncpg.UniqueViolationError) instead of a clean 409. Catching UniqueViolationError around the second UPDATE and re-raising HTTPException(409, 'Concurrent validation, retry.') would make this race-safe.

ai_propose_pricing strips markdown fences with a `.lstrip('json')` which is wrong — lstrip removes ANY of the chars in 'json', so a Claude response starting with the literal letter 'j', 'o', 's' or 'n' (e.g. 'json{...}' → after first strip becomes '{...}', but 'oranges 500' would become 'ranges 500'). In practice Claude always emits the exact 'json' prefix when it wraps so this works, but it's fragile. Use `.removeprefix('json').lstrip()` (Python 3.9+) instead.

ensure_pricing_ddl() is invoked on every pricing-related endpoint (manual, ai-propose, list, validate, reject, detail, AND on every /estimate + /request call). Same pattern as iter101's ensure_reviews_ddl, same 3-statement round-trip cost on every hit. Recommend hoisting all `ensure_*_ddl` calls into the FastAPI lifespan startup once.

PricingProposeRequest doesn't validate vehicle_type via Pydantic (no `Literal['standard','premium']` or pattern). The check happens in the route body — fine, but moving it into Pydantic would shave a request and harmonise with other admin schemas.

Backward compat: when the rider has no country_code on the users row, _resolve_pricing returns (None, 'XAF') hard-coded. That's correct for CM but wrong for any FR/NG rider without a country_code set yet — they'll be charged in XAF defaults regardless of the AI grid. Acceptable as a fallback but worth surfacing as a UX warning in the rider modal.
test_credentials: Same as memory/test_credentials.md. JWT minted via JWT_SECRET (HS256, sub+email+type=access).
seed_data_creation: Module-scope autouse fixture wipes pricing_grid rows for country_code IN ('CM','FR','XX','TS') at setup AND teardown. Also forces users.country_code='CM' for bob@japap.com so the /estimate grid path is exercised. No persistent test residue.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase B + Phase C backend GREEN. The Claude Sonnet 4.5 call via emergentintegrations.LlmChat with model ('anthropic','claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929') worked reliably across the full run. If the call ever returns invalid JSON, the route correctly emits HTTP 503 (test_09 has a pytest.skip guard for that rare case). Frontend (TransportPricingAdminTab.jsx, TransportOverviewAdminTab.jsx Recharts dashboard, TransportAdminTab wrapper with 3 sub-tabs) was NOT exercised — testing_type=backend + Cloudflare Turnstile blocks Playwright login on preview. To test FE, mint a JWT via backend/.env JWT_SECRET and inject as access_token cookie + Authorization header — same pattern as iter100/iter101.
rca_of_the_issue:
N/A — no failures other than a single transient 502 cold-start on the preview ingress, which recovered on first retry. Validated end-to-end:
  • AI proposal: real Claude call returns valid JSON with rationale; row inserted with status='proposed', source='ai', ai_rationale populated.
  • Manual proposal: admin-typed values stored exactly as sent; 422 on country_code != 2 chars; 422 on negative fare; 400 on invalid vehicle_type.
  • List: items + counts:{proposed,active,archived,rejected} + total; filters by country/status/vehicle_type; 400 on invalid status/vehicle.
  • Validate: 404 on missing pid; flips status='active'+sets validated_by/validated_at; ARCHIVES previous active row for same (country,vehicle_type) tuple — verified directly via GET; 409 on already-active or archived row.
  • Reject: 404; 200 on proposed → 'rejected' with rejected_reason+validated_by; 409 on already-rejected.
  • /estimate integration: pre-grid → pricing_source='default_xaf'+currency='XAF'+breakdown.base='500'; after grid validated → pricing_source='grid'+breakdown.base='1000'+per_km='400'; fare value changed accordingly.
  • Backward compat: CM/premium with no active grid still falls back to RATE_PREMIUM=350; admin without country_code falls back to default_xaf.
  • Phase C /admin/overview: 403 non-admin; 422 on days=0 / days>365; full shape with drivers (total/by_kyc_status with all 4 keys/online), rides (total/by_status with all 6 statuses), revenue (gross/commission/currency), timeseries (day/rides/gross/commission), top_earners (≤10, with earnings + completed_window), bottom_rated (≤10, all with total_reviews>=3, sorted by rating ASC). window_days echoed correctly for 1/7/30.
